packageRules.description: Group remaining npm devDependencies that are not already covered by more specific package groups. This rule should be early so specific rules can override it.
Chakra v2 requires @emotion as a peer dep — a version split may cause style failures.
Keep graphql core in sync with clients and codegen tools.
Keep ESLint plugins compatible with core.
Keep Sentry packages in sync.
Keep Next.js packages aligned.
Low-risk, high-frequency — consolidate to reduce noise.
Consolidate workflow action bumps
Keep Next server external workaround packages pinned to the transitive versions required by the current WalletConnect/pino stack.
Flag major bumps for manual review.
Block Chakra UI major upgrades while the repo is still on Chakra v2.
Block Emotion major upgrades while Chakra v2 is still in place.
Block ESLint ecosystem major bumps until we are ready to migrate the lint stack.
Block chakra-react-select v6 until we are ready for that migration.
Block Vitest v4 until we are ready to migrate the test tooling.
Block zod v4 until we are ready for that migration.
Block TypeScript major upgrades until moduleResolution and deep import issues are resolved.
Block @vitejs/plugin-react major upgrades — v5 is not compatible with 'moduleResolution': 'node'
Block vitest-mock-extended major upgrades — v4 requires vitest 4 which is not yet in the project.

Block @nktkas/hyperliquid updates. The library introduces frequent breaking changes (client redesigns, schema refactors, renamed parameters) between minor versions. Updating past 0.23.1 would require significant refactoring of packages/lib/modules/chains/hyperevm/useHyperEvm.ts.
Block pnpm major upgrades — v11 requires workspace-level changes (e.g. lockfile, CI images, engine checks) that need a dedicated migration.

Block dicebear major upgrades. v10 switched from @dicebear/collection to @dicebear/styles with JSON subpath exports that don't resolve with TypeScript moduleResolution: 'node'. Changing to 'bundler' breaks @sentry/nextjs deep imports.
Block GraphQL / Apollo major upgrades until the migration is handled separately.
